data.text/markdown:
NEM Capacity Expansion Model (CEM)

This model optimises when and how much generating capacity to build across the five NEM regions (QLD, NSW, VIC, SA, TAS) over four investment horizons: 2025, 2030, 2040, and 2050. The objective is to minimise the total system cost (capital + operating) discounted over the planning horizon subject to matching supply and demand at every time-step. Representative summer (December) and winter (June) weeks (28 + 28 × 6-hourly = 56 snapshots) are used to represent each investment period, with appropriate annual scaling weights. Investment periods and weights:
Period Duration Snapshot weight
2025 5 years 156.4 h/snapshot (28 summer + 28 winter × 6h)
2030 10 years 156.4 h/snapshot
2040 10 years 156.4 h/snapshot
2050 10 years 156.4 h/snapshot
Solve time: HiGHS typically takes a few minutes for this model locally. In MoLab these scenarios may run slowly depending on your internet connection — start the solve, then explore the controls.

data.text/html:
Carbon price is applied to coal (0.90 tCO₂/MWh) and gas (0.50 tCO₂/MWh) marginal costs.
Default retirements: Eraring 2027, Bayswater 2035, Mt Piper 2040, LoyYangA 2035, Callide C 2045, LoyYangB 2045, Tarong N 2037.
VPP = virtual power plant (aggregated home batteries, 2-hr duration). DSP = demand-side participation (flexible loads that can be deferred).
WACC affects the annualised capital cost of all new-entrant technologies.

Policy Constraints

Hard cap on whole-system CO₂ from electricity generation (coal + gas). Applied via PyPSA GlobalConstraint. Set to 0 to disable.
Minimum share of total NEM dispatch from solar, wind, offshore wind and hydro in each investment period. Applied per-period. Set to 0 to disable.
Capacity Investment Scheme (CIS) style floors: minimum total extendable renewable (or storage) capacity active in each period across the NEM. Set to 0 to disable.
